channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Concerns Grow Over ADF Morale Amid Policy Uncertainty and Recruitment Challenges
item.description: As Australia marks Anzac Day, concerns are mounting over the state of the Australian Defence Force. Historical lessons from Gallipoli underscore the importance of proper preparation and support for mi...

item.title: Lagos Reintroduces Monthly Sanitation Exercise, Enforces Compliance
item.description: Lagos State Government don tok sey dem dey still go ahead with di monthly sanitation exercise wey dey come up on Saturday. Dem say no court don give judgement wey go stop di exercise, so everybody mus...

item.title: Revised UK government estimates show AI datacentres could cause millions of tonnes in carbon emissions
item.description: Recent revisions to the UK's estimate of carbon emissions from artificial intelligence (AI) datacentres have sparked alarm as officials now believe the emissions could reach up to 123 million tonnes o...

item.title: A Man’s Reflection on Gender Dynamics in Gyms
item.description: In this opinion piece, Ian Warden, a male columnist, explores his frustration with the behaviour of men in gyms, contrasting it with the experience of women in women-only fitness spaces. Warden expres...

item.title: Sindh government distributes initial compensation to Gul Plaza fire victims, announces Rs7bn relief plan
item.description: The Sindh government has started compensating traders affected by the devastating January 17 fire at Karachi’s Gul Plaza, with Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah distributing Rs511.7 million among 200 shop...

item.title: Community responds following the acquittal of Jeffrey Sloka
item.description: Following the acquittal of Jeffrey Sloka, reactions from the Kitchener community and advocacy groups have been mixed. Many members of the public expressed disappointment and frustration, citing concer...

item.title: Continued Violence Casts Doubt on Lebanon-Israel Truce Extension
item.description: The recent extension of the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is facing challenges as Hezbollah continues to assert that the truce is essentially ‘meaningless’ and fighting persists. The three-week...

item.title: Mike Vrabel Takes Time Off for Counseling Amid Personal Matters
item.description: Newly released photos have drawn attention to New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and NFL reporter Dianna Russini, showing the two together at a New York City bar in 2020 and at a Mississippi ...

item.title: Review of Japanese and Korean dining experiences in Durban and Cape Town
item.description: This piece follows two separate dining experiences that explore Asian-inspired cuisine in South Africa, one in Durban North and the other in Cape Town. The first visit takes place at The Green Parrot,...

item.title: Teen extradited from Dubai charged over 2024 fatal stabbing of woman in London Hyde Park home
item.description: An 18-year-old man, Enzo Bettamio, has been extradited from Dubai to the United Kingdom and formally charged with the murder of Kamonnan Thiamphanit, a 27-year-old Thai national, who was found fatally...

item.title: Why South African museums must evolve into inclusive, living spaces
item.description: More than three decades into South Africa’s democracy, Jane Mufamadi argues that museums and heritage institutions need to shift from static, object-focused spaces into dynamic environments that activ...

item.title: Sara Duterte commends Army actions in Negros Occidental encounter as officials seek investigation
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte expressed support for the Philippine Army following a reported clash in Negros Occidental involving government forces and suspected members of the New People’s Army (NPA). ...

item.title: New Zealand pushes for revival of mid-year Anzac Test against Australia
item.description: New Zealand players and officials are advocating for the return of the mid-year Anzac Test against Australia, aiming to create a meaningful fixture outside the end-of-year internationals. The last Anz...

item.title: Gatineau bylaw complaint prompts warning over backyard bird feeder
item.description: A Gatineau, Quebec, resident is reconsidering her backyard bird feeder after receiving a warning from the city that it could result in a $400 fine. Ashley Robillard installed the feeder earlier in Apr...

item.title: Supreme Court allows medical termination for minor, emphasises woman's choice
item.description: In a significant judgment, the Supreme Court of India has allowed the medical termination of a pregnancy beyond seven months for a 15-year-old girl. The bench, consisting of Justices B V Nagarathna an...

item.title: Iran rules out direct talks with U.S. during Pakistan visit as indirect diplomacy continues
item.description: The ongoing tensions linked to the Israel-Iran conflict and wider U.S. involvement continue to evolve, with fresh diplomatic developments emerging from Pakistan. Iran has clearly stated that it will n...

item.title: Seven AAP MPs Resign, Three Join BJP Amid Organisational Issues
item.description: In a significant political development, seven Members of Parliament from the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) resigned, with three of them subsequently joining the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). The departures, t...

item.title: Google strengthens partnership with Anthropic with potential $40 billion investment
item.description: Alphabet, the parent company of Google, has announced a substantial investment in Anthropic, an artificial intelligence startup that has been gaining recognition for its model focused on coding. Initi...

item.title: Paramount executives meet Trump and DOJ official ahead of Warner Bros. Discovery acquisition review
item.description: Executives from Paramount and Skydance hosted a private dinner in Washington D.C. with former President Donald Trump and senior administration officials, including Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche...

item.title: Akira of BGYO Expresses Pride in BINI's Successful Coachella Performance
item.description: Akira, a member of the Filipino boy band BGYO, took to social media to congratulate the Filipino girl group BINI for their remarkable performance at Coachella 2026. Expressing his immense pride, Akira...
